title: BIM for Airport Projects: Multi-Discipline Coordination at Scale
content:
Airport projects combine civil, structural, architectural, MEP, baggage handling, passenger information, and security systems BIM in a single coordination environment. The scale, phasing complexity, and public safety requirements of airport BIM make coordination discipline essential from project start.

Why This Matters

Airport projects that fail at BIM coordination produce delays that affect airline operations, passenger experience, and regulatory compliance. The cost of a coordination failure discovered during fit-out of a baggage hall or security checkpoint is amplified by the operational impact of delayed terminal opening.

Practical Guidance

Federated Model Scale: Airport terminal BIM models are among the largest in the industry. Manage federated model size by splitting the model by terminal zone and level, using Navisworks as the coordination platform rather than attempting to open all models in Revit simultaneously.

Baggage System BIM: Baggage handling systems — conveyors, sorters, check-in belts, claim carousels — are specialist MEP that require BIM coordination with structural support frames, pit openings, and MEP systems. Define the baggage system BIM interface with the specialist contractor in the BIM Execution Plan.

Phased Operations: Airport expansions require phased construction while the airport remains operational. 4D BIM for airport projects must model temporary works, hoarding, and operational boundaries alongside permanent construction to identify phasing conflicts before site mobilisation.

Security and Resilience: Airport BIM includes security zones, access control points, and resilience infrastructure that must not appear in publicly accessible models. Establish data security protocols for airport BIM that define which model elements are security-classified and restrict access accordingly.

Checklist

  • Split federated model by zone and level for performance management
  • Define baggage system BIM interface with specialist contractor in BEP
  • Include operational boundary modeling in 4D BIM for phased construction
  • Establish data security protocols for security-classified model elements

LUA BIM LABS Insight

Airport BIM scale and security requirements add layers of coordination discipline to an already complex multi-system environment — the BIM Execution Plan must address both from project day one.
